title: Modelo de aprobación humana y persistencia operacional
wave: Ola 3 — Seguridad operacional, aprobación humana y ejecución controlada

architectural_decision.reason: FUNC-SPRINT-28 extends the existing local SQLite persistence layer and adds a dependency-free approval domain. It introduces no external dependency, network call, provider, UI, destructive action or new storage backend. The existing LocalStore is migrated idempotently.

pass_criteria: ApprovalRecord has unique ID, subject, tool/action, status, actor, reason, scope, created_at and expires_at.
Allowed states are requested, approved, denied, revoked and expired.
Existing SQLite approvals tables are migrated without dropping rows.
block_criteria: An approval lacks expiration or scope.
An approval can be overwritten without a controlled transition.
The approvals table breaks compatibility with an existing local DB.
Sprint 28 connects approvals to critical execution before policy binding.
risks: The approval actor is local/declarative; RBAC is future work.
PolicyEngine binding is explicitly deferred to Sprint 30.
CLI approval operations are explicitly deferred to Sprint 29.
Event persistence is best-effort until later operational hardening.
next_sprint: FUNC-SPRINT-29 — CLI de aprobación: request, list, show, approve, deny y revoke
